Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly describes methylation of hydroxyl groups using SAM and specifically shows/directly states methylation of tetrahydrocolumbamine to tetrahydropalmatine by CjColOMT.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: In vivo yeast expression increased tetrahydropalmatine and also supported armepavine and laudanine production.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four statements are present in Results/Discussion: BridgIT prediction and validation, ~8-fold increase in vivo, >11-fold higher in vitro conversion than yPsS9OMT, and additional activities on multiple BIA substrates.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Text states CjColOMT was codon optimized, cloned into a high-copy plasmid, and transformed into engineered S. cerevisiae.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Directly supported as an enzyme module for heterologous microbial production of tetrahydropalmatine and other derivatives; paper discusses further optimization/protein engineering.
